source: U.S. BLS, CPI-U (U.S. city average, NSA): six major groups with continuous history since 1967 + All items. Public domain.
definition: Localized inflation = relative-price dispersion. dispersion is the cross-sectional std dev of the six groups' 12-month % changes; spread is hottest − coldest; skew is Pearson moment skewness. Ball & Mankiw (1995): positive skew (a few categories spiking up) is the fingerprint of an adverse supply shock and pulls headline inflation up.
